key_insight: A physics-informed neural network can train turbulence closures in a solver-agnostic way, making them generalize better across bluff-body shapes than conventional inverse calibration.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast screening and deployment of turbulence models that are more portable across CFD solvers and practical for engineering flows where DNS is unavailable.
method: Impose the RANS residual directly in a PINN using automatic differentiation, avoiding mesh-dependent inverse optimization.
Train four closure variants: three Reynolds-stress models with realizability constraints and one direct force model without those constraints.
Use input-gradient smoothing and a Lipschitz constraint to improve coupled solver stability before freezing the learned closure in a finite-element code.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on six 2D bluff-body wakes at Re = 10^4, then tested with in-sample and leave-one-shape-out evaluation; the direct force model achieved the best out-of-sample performance with about 8.5% drag error.
watch_item: Although promising, the learned closures still need solver-stable deployment and their performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the training geometries and PIV/DNS data.
paper_title: Generalizable turbulence closures across bluff-body shapes by PINN-based solver-agnostic training
